title: Merchant account rules
description: Control when each merchant account appears on the terminal, so your team never charges to the wrong account.
semantics: An account WITHOUT a rule is always shown. With a rule, it only appears when ALL of its conditions are met. If exactly one account remains eligible, the terminal picks it automatically.
fallbackInfo: Safety net: if NO account meets the rules, the terminal shows all of them with a warning — a rule never blocks a sale. Every occurrence is logged in the audit trail.
empty: This business has no merchant accounts configured yet. Contact support to add them.
deleted: Rule for {{name}} removed — the account is always shown again
needOneCondition: Enable at least one condition to save.

conditions.scheduleHint: Show this account only on certain days and hours (business local time).
conditions.midnightHint: A window may cross midnight (e.g. 22:00 – 02:00); it belongs to the day it starts.
conditions.geofenceHint: Show it only when the terminal is within a radius around a point.
conditions.volumeCapHint: Hide it after reaching an amount or number of charges in the period. It hides BEFORE exceeding the cap (the charge in progress counts).
conditions.ticketAmountHint: Show it only for tickets within an amount range.
conditions.staffHint: Show it only when certain team roles are charging.
conditions.circuitBreakerHint: If the account fails several charges in a row, the terminal hides it for a while and retries later.

simulator.title: Simulator
simulator.description: Test what the terminal would show for a charge — it uses the exact same engine.
simulator.featureInactive: Rules are not active for this business: the terminal shows every account, as usual.
simulator.fallback: No account meets the rules with these inputs: the terminal would show ALL of them with a warning (the sale is not blocked).
simulator.autoSelect: The terminal would auto-select: {{name}} (only eligible account — no selection screen).
